topic: Langflow vs n8n vs Dify: Which AI Workflow Tool Should Developers Choose?
keyword: Langflow vs n8n vs Dify comparison

key_points: Comprehensive 2026 comparison of n8n, Dify, LangGraph, Coze, RAGFlow
Positions Dify/FastGPT as complete platforms for low-barrier AI app building
n8n sits in between as general workflow tool with added AI modules
LangGraph/Fabric are developer frameworks for maximum flexibility
Dify wins for LLM-powered SaaS with multiple teams and long-lived projects
Langflow wins when team already runs LangChain in production
Dify has full LLM-app platform: backend, database, admin UI, API gateway, prompt management
Langflow better for prototyping before exporting to code
n8n focuses on general-purpose automation connecting apps and APIs (200+ integrations)
Langflow designed specifically for LLM/AI workflows using LangChain ecosystem
Both are open source but serve fundamentally different primary use cases
Langflow better for RAG pipelines; n8n better for business process automation with AI
n8n better for teams whose AI work is really an automation/orchestration problem
Langflow better for teams building complex AI agents with LangGraph
n8n's strength in retries, branches, schedules, and clear run logs
Langflow excels in visual debugging of RAG pipelines
Dify leads in raw processing capacity and production-ready enterprise features
Langflow offers more flexible vector database integrations
Dify has stronger team management and built-in app publishing system
Langflow more suitable for developers wanting Python flexibility and code export

stats.fact: Langflow hit 100k GitHub stars milestone in August 2025, growing 300% year-over-year; 1.2M monthly PyPI downloads and 15k daily active users
Dify has 114K-131K GitHub stars as of early 2026 and raised $30M Series Pre-A funding
n8n crossed 180,000 GitHub stars as of April 2026, making it one of the most starred workflow automation repos
Workflow automation market valued at $26.01 billion in 2026, projected to reach $40.77 billion by 2031 at 9.41% CAGR
51% of companies have already deployed AI agents; 79% report some form of AI agent adoption within their organizations
40% of enterprise applications will include task-specific AI agents by end of 2026, up from less than 5% in 2025
n8n Cloud starts at $24/mo for 2,500 executions; Dify Professional at $59/mo; Langflow is free open source with hosting costs $20-500/mo
SMB AI adoption jumped from 22% (2024) to 38% (2026), nearly doubling in two years
Dify supports 100+ LLM models; Langflow supports all major LLMs via LangChain ecosystem; n8n has 200+ pre-built third-party integrations

angles: The practical 'which tool do you actually ship to production?' test — real developer decision criteria
The automation vs. application vs. orchestration split: each tool owns a different layer of the AI stack
When combining all three makes sense vs. picking one — a hybrid architecture perspective
True total cost of ownership in 2026: beyond GitHub stars and feature lists
Reading the market signals: Langflow/IBM acquisition, Dify's $30M raise, n8n's growth — what they mean for developer lock-in
RAG pipeline showdown: which tool delivers best vector search + LLM integration for production apps
keywords: best AI workflow tool for developers 2026
dify vs langflow which is better
